title: Commercial Quantum Backend Record
description: Investor-grade record for a single commercially available or announced quantum computing backend. Every numeric value is expected to be traceable via the `sources` array. Unknown values MUST be the string 'Not publicly disclosed', never invented.

provValue.description: A value carrying its own provenance.

source.description: Canonical source URL for this value.

retrieved.description: ISO-8601 date/time the value was retrieved.

kind.description: Whether the value is a direct vendor spec, from a calibration API, or from a publication.

unknown.description: Placeholder for values that could not be found.

id.description: Stable identifier for this backend; derived from vendor+backend_name when not set explicitly.

black_box.description: Black-box benchmark capability score (e.g. algorithmic qubits / #AQ, application benchmark). Define per record what was measured via `method`.

argmax.description: Best single reported headline metric the vendor leads with, verbatim (e.g. '#AQ 64', 'QV 2^20'). Used for quick comparison only.

vendor_metric.description: The vendor's own preferred figure of merit and its value (e.g. IonQ Algorithmic Qubits, IBM Quantum Volume/CLOPS, Quantinuum Quantum Volume).

theoretical_max.description: Derived headroom metric. See derived_metrics.theoretical_max for the reproducible inputs and formula; this field mirrors the numeric value with provenance kind='computed'.

type.description: e.g. heavy-hex, all-to-all, square-lattice, linear, reconfigurable-neutral-atom

derived_metrics.description: Values computed by this pipeline, with reproducible inputs.

fidelity_stats.description: Populated only when the vendor exposes per-gate calibration data.

field.description: Dotted path of the field this source backs, e.g. 'fidelity.2q_avg'.
